pending.body: `src/cli/project.rs:634-663` only special-cases `RunStatus::NotStarted` when an existing project already owns the same bead. If the prior bead-backed project is in `failed`, `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation()` returns `Ok(())`, so `execute_create_from_bead()` falls through to `claim_bead_in_br` instead of forcing the operator back to `ralph-burning project select <id>` + `ralph-burning run resume`. That violates the bead's retry policy for failed/stopped attempts and can still yield a parallel task or an unrelated BR-claim failure. Failed same-bead projects need the same targeted create-time rejection/resume guidance as the active-lineage helper.

`src/cli/project.rs:444-446` relies on the new task-runs lock to make duplicate-bead validation atomic, but `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:413-425` and `3140-3147` still clear `task-runs.ndjson` directly during pending-lineage-reset handling without taking that same lock. That path is reachable from `record_bead_start()` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3172-3175`, so a concurrent lineage-resetting operation can rewrite the file while `create-from-bead` believes it has exclusive access. The new lock therefore does not actually close the TOCTOU window it was added for; lineage reset needs to go through the lineage store or acquire the task-runs lock before truncating the file.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 9 -> Plan and Implement
